yield_notes: LAI of sole cropped cassava reached 3.7 at 150 DAP, which was greater than the LAI of 2.0 to 3.0 needed for maximum storage root growth.
LAI of intercropped cassava was lower than sole cropped cassava, but within the desired range for storage root growth.
More dry matter than sole cropped cassava between 50 and 105 DAP.
Yield reduction when intercropped with peanut, associated with 0.8 reduction in LAI and 4.4 fewer storage roots per plant.
More dry matter produced by sole cropped cassava than intercropped cassava at harvest.
Dry matter accumulation in storage roots of sole cropped cassava at final harvest.
Dry matter accumulation in storage roots of intercropped cassava at final harvest.
Less pod dry matter produced by intercropped cowpea than sole cropped cowpea at 80 DAP.
LAI reduction of peanut when intercropped with cassava at 65 DAP.
